title: fix #17244: use /etc/timezone where `timedatectl` is missing on Linux
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body:
> @nabbisen you must sign your commit.
> 
> git commit -a --amend -s git push --force

Done.
Excuse me. I missed the last of [Sign your PRs](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs).
Thank you so much.

Could you amend and repush again, so that the CI/CD system will not require tests.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

> Could you amend and repush again, so that the CI/CD system will not require tests.
> 
> git commit -a --amend -s git push --force

Sure. Done.

Readability improved.

```diff
- 	var output []byte
- 	var err error
- 	if output, err = exec.Command("timedatectl", "show", "--property=Timezone").Output(); errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
+ 	output, err := exec.Command("timedatectl", "show", "--property=Timezone").Output()
+ 	if errors.Is(err, exec.ErrNotFound) {
```
